steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content: Exactly one distinct real solution means the quadratic can be written as a perfect square equal to $0$. Check which choice has a repeated factor.

steps.title: Identify the repeated-root choice
steps.content: Choice A crosses the x-axis twice, choice B never reaches the x-axis, and choice C crosses twice. The correct answer is $y=x^2-16x+64=(x-8)^2$, which touches the x-axis once at $x=8$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Because $(x-8)^2=0$ has exactly one solution, $x=8$, D is correct.
